summary: March PPI — released this morning — follows February's final demand index rising 3.4% year-over-year, keeping producer-level inflation firmly in focus. Inflation remains too high to achieve the Fed's 2% target anytime soon, dimming hopes for rate cuts in 2026, which is driving gold's outperformance and the dollar's slide.
tweet: March PPI lands today against a 3.4% Feb YoY baseline, while major bank Q1 earnings stress-test whether fundamentals can hold up under sticky inflation and tariff headwinds.
